subsectors.name: Fiscal Management
subsectors.description: Maximized resource mobilization and utilization through enhanced financial management systems (for real-time reporting, ensured fiscal transparency and accountability, for the efficient delivery of public services.)
milestones: Integrated Financial Management System (IFMS) operationalized province-wide and half of tax declarations digitalized into a centralized data bank.
Fiscal transparency, participatory monitoring, and full tax declaration digitalization achieved through a public portal and modernized financial systems.
Automated financial controls institutionalized within the IFMS, enabling real-time validation, monitoring, and system-enforced compliance.
Data-driven resource mobilization institutionalized through predictive analytics, with significantly reduced audit findings reflecting mature fiscal discipline.
Maximized resource mobilization and utilization through enhanced financial management systems
kpis:
• 100% of PLGU departments using IFMS
• ≥ 50% of component LGUs onboarded
• ≥ 50% of tax declarations digitalized
• IFMS uptime ≥ 95%
• ≥ 60% reduction in paper-based financial transactions

• 100% of key financial reports on public portal
• 100% of tax declarations digitalized
• Public fiscal portal operational with search, download, and feedback
• ≥ 80% citizen satisfaction on fiscal transparency
• Online taxpayer self-service fully operational

• 100% of core financial controls automated
• 100% of fund releases through automated validation
• 100% of procurement monitored in real time
• ≥ 80% reduction in manual reconciliation errors
• 100% system-generated audit trail coverage

• ≥ 3 new revenue streams operationalized
• ≥ 25% increase in Local Source Revenue from analytics-driven strategies
• ≥ 70% reduction in COA AOMs from 2025 baseline
• 100% of fiscal decisions supported by predictive analytics
• Fiscal Risk Management Framework institutionalized

• 100% real-time fiscal reporting and public disclosure
• Sustained unqualified COA opinion for 10+ consecutive years
• Fiscal autonomy ratio (LSR/Total Income) ≥ 50%
• Province recognized as national fiscal governance model

sectors.vision: A resilient, inclusive, and people-centered province with reduced poverty, healthy and educated citizens, and empowered communities supported by accessible, high-quality, and future-ready social services.

subsectors.name: Health
subsectors.description: Healthy and resilient communities with accessible, quality, and responsive healthcare services.
milestones:
Strengthened primary and preventive healthcare established across all municipalities, with disaster-ready health workforce and accredited health facilities province-wide.

Upgrading of district hospitals
Universal primary healthcare access, digital health records, and adequate medical workforce achieved province-wide, including priority coverage for aging and geographically isolated populations
AI-enabled disease surveillance, telemedicine, and strengthened workforce deployment operational, with near-universal rural primary care coverage.
Universal, high-quality, and internationally recognized healthcare achieved, with preventable hospitalizations nearly eliminated and no community left behind.
Healthy and resilient communities with accessible, quality, and responsive healthcare services.
kpis:
• Percentage of municipalities with fully operational primary healthcare facilities (Target: 100%)
• Percentage of provincial health facilities with current PhilHealth accreditation (Target: 100%)
• Number of health workers trained and certified under disaster-ready health workforce programs
• Percentage of municipalities with functional Emergency Response Teams (ERTs) integrated into the health system

• Percentage of population with access to primary healthcare services within prescribed distance or travel time (Target: 100%)
• Percentage of health facilities with fully operational digital health records system (Target: 100%)
• Doctor-to-population and nurse-to-population ratios against WHO and DOH benchmarks (Target: at or above standard)
• Percentage of geographically isolated and disadvantaged areas (GIDAs) and senior citizen populations covered by priority healthcare programs

•  AI-enabled disease surveillance system operational and generating real-time alerts (Target: system live with alert response rate tracked)
•  Number of municipalities and barangays covered by telemedicine services
•  Percentage of rural barangays with access to primary care services through telemedicine or deployed health workforce (Target: ≥90%)
•  Number of disease outbreaks detected and contained through AI surveillance within prescribed response time

•  Preventable hospitalization rate per 100,000 population (Target: near-zero, defined threshold)
•  Number of provincial health facilities with international quality certification or recognition
•  Percentage of barangays with zero unserved population in primary healthcare (Target: 100%)
•  Maternal mortality rate and under-five mortality rate against international benchmarks (Target: at or below defined threshold)

•  Overall health system performance index score (composite of access, quality, and responsiveness indicators)
•  Percentage of provincial population covered by comprehensive health insurance or social health protection (Target: 100%)
•  Life expectancy at birth against national and regional benchmarks (Target: at or above)
•  Percentage of health facilities sustaining accreditation and quality certification without lapse

subsectors.name: Social Welfare and Development
subsectors.description: Reduced poverty and vulnerability through inclusive and responsive social protection and development services.
milestones: Reduced poverty, expanded livelihood opportunities, and decreased youth delinquency achieved through district-level livelihood infrastructure and strengthened social protection services.
Safer communities achieved through substantial crime reduction and near-universal drug-cleared status of barangays.

Digitalized social welfare delivery, evidence-based community interventions, and institutionalized social welfare reforms operationalized, providing long-term support to recovering and vulnerable populations.
Full coverage of basic social welfare services across all barangays achieved through AI-powered tools, with a provincial resilience and empowerment index established.
Reduced poverty and vulnerability through inclusive and responsive social protection and development services.
kpis:
• Poverty incidence rate reduction compared to baseline (Target: measurable and defined reduction)
• Number of district-level livelihood infrastructure facilities established and operational
• Number of beneficiaries enrolled in livelihood and skills development programs
• Youth delinquency rate reduction compared to baseline (Target: measurable and defined reduction)

• Percentage reduction in crime volume compared to 2028 baseline (Target: substantial and defined reduction)
• Percentage of barangays with drug-cleared status (Target: near-universal, ≥90%)
• Number of recovering drug dependents provided with sustained social welfare and reintegration support
• Percentage of social protection beneficiaries receiving complete package of services within the period

• % of social welfare services delivered through a digitalized management and tracking system (Target: majority, ≥60%)
• Number of evidence-based community intervention programs implemented with documented outcome evaluations
• Number of social welfare reform policies institutionalized through provincial ordinance or executive order
• % of vulnerable population groups (PWDs, senior citizens, women in crisis) with active case management records in the digital system

• % of barangays with full coverage of basic social welfare services (Target: 100%)
• Provincial Resilience and Empowerment Index established and baseline score recorded (Target: index operational)
• Number of AI-powered tools deployed in social welfare service delivery and case management
• % of improvement in provincial resilience and empowerment index score from baseline

• Poverty incidence rate against national and regional targets (Target: at or below defined threshold)
• Percentage of identified vulnerable households covered by at least one active social protection program (Target: 100%)
• Social welfare service satisfaction rating from beneficiary survey (Target: defined threshold)
• Provincial Resilience and Empowerment Index score at target maturity level

subsectors.description: Educated, skilled, and future-ready citizens with equitable access to quality learning opportunities.
milestones: Equitable access to quality education expanded through improved completion rates, new community learning institutions, scholarship growth, and the province's first digital library infrastructure.
Scholarship coverage doubled, digital learning infrastructure expanded to the mainland, and a significant proportion of teachers equipped with AI integration and digital pedagogy competencies.

Majority of teachers competent in AI and digital pedagogy, virtual learning infrastructure extended to Siargao Islands, and the province actively engaged in international academic and cultural exchange networks.

Educated, skilled, and future-ready citizens with equitable access to quality learning opportunities.

• Number of institutionalized international academic and cultural exchange partnerships (Target: robust network, defined threshold)
• Dropout rate across all school levels (Target: near-zero, defined threshold)

• Literacy rate against national benchmark (Target: at or above)
• Percentage of graduates transitioning to employment, entrepreneurship, or higher education within one year (Target: defined threshold)
• Education equity index score across municipalities including GIDAs and island communities (Target: highest equity level)
• Percentage of schools sustaining digital learning infrastructure and teacher competency standards without lapse

programs.name: Cooperative Compliance and Governance Strengthening Program
programs.description: Conduct Compliance Clinics with CDA and LGUs, develop and distribute a Coop Governance Toolkit (digital templates and manuals), and create online reporting portals for easier CDA compliance submission across all registered cooperatives province-wide. The CDA Liaison Office shall serve as lead implementing office in coordination with component LGU cooperative focal persons.

programs.name: Cooperative Leadership and Governance Training Program
programs.description: Launch a Leadership and Governance Training Series for cooperative officers and organize regional orientation seminars for newly registered cooperatives, covering governance, financial management, and statutory compliance standards. The CDA Liaison Office shall serve as lead office in coordination with PPDO.

policies.name: Mandatory Compliance Orientation Policy
policies.description: Require all newly registered and existing cooperatives to complete a CDA-prescribed compliance orientation covering governance, financial reporting, and statutory requirements as a condition for provincial cooperative program participation and incentive eligibility.

policies.name: Digital Reporting Policy
policies.description: Mandate all cooperatives receiving provincial support to submit compliance and financial reports through the provincial online reporting portal, with the CDA Liaison Office serving as system administrator and compliance monitor.

programs.name: Cooperative Financing and Capitalization Program
programs.description: Implement low-interest loan and matching grant programs for cooperatives in coordination with LandBank, DBP, and CDA, and establish Regional Cooperative Credit Bureaus to strengthen member financial accountability and creditworthiness. The CDA Liaison Office shall serve as lead office in coordination with PBO and PPDO.

programs.name: Financial Literacy and Enterprise Development Training Program
programs.description: Conduct province-wide Financial Literacy Campaigns for cooperative members and leaders, and roll out Enterprise Development Training covering marketing, bookkeeping, and cost control targeting at least 70% of registered cooperatives by end of 2031. The CDA Liaison Office shall serve as lead office in coordination with TESDA-SDN and DTI-SDN.

programs.name: Cooperative Insurance and Guarantee Scheme Pilot Program
programs.description: Pilot Coop Insurance and Guarantee Schemes in priority cooperatives to reduce lending risks and expand access to formal credit, in coordination with PCIC, PhilGuarantee, and accredited insurance providers. The CDA Liaison Office shall serve as lead office.

policies.name: Coop Credit Access Policy
policies.description: Direct the provincial government to facilitate and co-guarantee cooperative access to formal credit through LandBank, DBP, CDA revolving funds, and microfinance institutions, with the PBO and CDA Liaison Office jointly monitoring credit program utilization and repayment performance.

policies.name: Enterprise Development Policy
policies.description: Mandate all cooperatives receiving provincial financial support to submit and implement Enterprise Development Plans covering marketing strategy, cost efficiency measures, and revenue diversification within 12 months of fund release, with the CDA Liaison Office monitoring compliance.

programs.name: Coop Trade Hubs and Annual Cooperative Expo Program
programs.description: Launch provincial Coop Trade Hubs as permanent market access facilities for cooperative products and services, and organize an Annual Cooperative Expo showcasing provincial cooperative products to local, regional, and national buyers. The CDA Liaison Office shall serve as lead office in coordination with DTI-SDN and component LGUs.

programs.name: Digital Marketing, E-Commerce, and Coop-to-Coop Networking Program
programs.description: Support Digital Marketing and E-Commerce Adoption for cooperatives through platform training and digital marketplace enrollment, and facilitate Coop-to-Coop Networking Activities forming supply and value chain clusters across municipalities. The CDA Liaison Office shall serve as lead office in coordination with DICT-SDN and DTI-SDN.

programs.name: Export Readiness Training Program
programs.description: Provide Export Readiness Training for cooperatives with identified export potential, covering product standards, packaging, export documentation, and international market access pathways in coordination with DA Caraga, BFAR Caraga, and DTI-SDN. The CDA Liaison Office shall serve as lead office.

policies.name: Inclusive Procurement Policy
policies.description: Require provincial government departments and component LGUs to prioritize cooperative-produced goods and services in government procurement processes compliant with RA 9184, with the BAC Secretariat and CDA Liaison Office jointly monitoring cooperative procurement participation rates.

policies.name: Digital Market Access Policy
policies.description: Mandate all cooperatives receiving provincial trade support to register on at least one provincial or national digital marketplace platform, with the MISO and CDA Liaison Office providing technical onboarding assistance and monitoring digital sales performance.

programs.name: Coop Research and Development Centers Program
programs.description: Establish Coop R&D Centers in partnership with SUCs — particularly SNSU and Caraga State University — supporting applied research on cooperative business models, green technologies, and market innovations relevant to Surigao del Norte's priority sectors. The CDA Liaison Office shall serve as lead office in coordination with CHED Caraga and partner SUCs.

programs.name: Coop Innovation and Technology Grants Program
programs.description: Roll out Coop Innovation and Technology Grants funding cooperative adoption of automation, renewable energy, AI tools, and blockchain-based supply chain transparency, targeting financial self-sustainability for at least 75% of registered cooperatives by end of 2037. The CDA Liaison Office shall serve as lead office in coordination with DOST-SDN and DTI-SDN.

programs.name: Industry 4.0 Skills Training and Green Cooperative Program
programs.description: Train cooperative members in Industry 4.0 competencies (digital tools, e-commerce, data analytics) and implement the Green Cooperative Program supporting eco-tourism, organic farming, and clean energy cooperatives, with training delivered through the Coop R&D Centers in partnership with SUCs. The CDA Liaison Office shall serve as lead office in coordination with TESDA-SDN and PENRO.

programs.name: Cooperative Innovation Summit Program
programs.description: Organize annual Cooperative Innovation Summits for provincial and regional knowledge sharing on cooperative best practices, technology adoption, and green business innovations, positioning Surigao del Norte cooperatives as regional models for sustainable enterprise development. The CDA Liaison Office shall serve as lead office in coordination with CDA Regional Office and PPDO.

policies.name: Innovation Incentive Policy
policies.description: Provide provincial fiscal incentives — including local tax reductions and priority access to provincial grants — for cooperatives adopting certified green business practices, Industry 4.0 technologies, or renewable energy systems, with the CDA Liaison Office and PTO jointly administering the incentive program.

policies.name: Coop–SUC Partnership Policy
policies.description: Mandate the CDA Liaison Office to formalize MOAs between provincial cooperatives and at least two SUCs for R&D collaboration, technology transfer, student practicum hosting, and joint enterprise development, with partnerships reviewed and renewed every three years.

programs.name: National Cooperative Enterprise Network and Global Trade Linkages Program
programs.description: Form the National Cooperative Enterprise Network (NCEN) for provincial cooperative branding and global trade, and develop a Global Trade Linkages Program facilitating cooperative exports, joint ventures, and international partnerships

programs.name: Coop Leaders Academy and Succession Planning Program
programs.description: Institutionalize the Coop Leaders Academy providing advanced governance training and succession planning for cooperative officers, ensuring leadership continuity and institutional resilience across all generations of cooperative membership

programs.name: Cooperative Member Social Protection Scheme Program
programs.description: Launch comprehensive Social Protection Schemes for cooperative members

programs.name: Public-Private-People Partnership (4Ps) Inclusive Community Development Sustainment Program
programs.description: Sustain Public-Private-People Partnership projects linking cooperatives, LGUs, private enterprises, and community organizations for inclusive community development

policies.name: Global Market Integration Policy
policies.description: Direct the provincial government to actively facilitate provincial cooperative access to national and international markets

policies.name: Social Protection Policy
policies.description: Mandate all cooperatives with 50 or more active members to enroll members in at least one PhilHealth, SSS, or accredited insurance program as a condition for continued provincial incentive eligibility

policies.name: Public-Private-People Partnership Policy (4Ps)
policies.description: Institutionalize 4Ps as a permanent provincial cooperative development strategy
